display.purpose: One per page, top-left. Never exceeds 24px/700 — see The No-Display-Font Rule.

headline.purpose: Card and modal headings within a page.

body.purpose: Table cells, form labels, paragraph copy, button labels — the workhorse size.

label.purpose: Status badges, table meta, helper text.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Default resting state for every card, table container, filter panel.

motion.name: ease-out-strong
motion.purpose: Standard entrance/exit curve for modals, toasts, dropdowns, mount-fades — established 2026-07-16 across the app, replacing the weaker Tailwind built-in ease-out.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2563eb; color: #ffffff; padding: 8px 20px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; transition: background-color 150ms, transform 100ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1d4ed8; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #60a5fa; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Ghost Button
components.description: Destructive action trigger (delete), always paired with a confirm dialog before the action fires.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: transparent; color: #dc2626; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid #fecaca; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: background-color 150ms, transform 100ms; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #fef2f2; } .ds-btn-danger:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Success Badge
components.description: Status label for completed / clean items.
components.css: .ds-badge-success { background: #f0fdf4; color: #15803d; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Danger Badge
components.description: Status label for flagged / failed / scam items.
components.css: .ds-badge-error { background: #fef2f2; color: #b91c1c; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text/search field used across filter bars and forms.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #ffffff; color: #1f2937; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 150ms, box-shadow 150ms; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #60a5fa; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(96,165,250,0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default content container: tables, filter panels, detail sections.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 1px 2px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); padding: 20px; }

narrative.overview: This is the screen a moderator has open all shift: a duty desk, not a storefront. Every surface exists to get someone from "what happened" to "what do I do about it" as fast as possible — flagged calls, raw transcripts, speaker identities, platform settings, an audit trail. Nothing here is trying to be liked; it's trying to be trusted and fast. The system explicitly rejects the playful, bright SaaS-marketing look: no gradient heroes, no bouncy celebratory motion, no oversized display type standing in for a value proposition. The product itself exists to catch manipulation and urgency in scam calls — its own interface refuses to use those same tricks on the people running it. Density is currently light-to-medium: generous white space around card-based sections, one soft shadow tier, a single blue accent reserved for actions.
keyCharacteristics: One primary accent (blue), reserved for actions and links — never used decoratively.
Three status colors (red / green / amber) carry real state, not mood.
Flat, single-shadow cards throughout; no elevation hierarchy yet.
System sans-serif everywhere; no display or brand typeface.
Dense information (tables, filters, timestamps) over illustration or imagery.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Working Blue is the only color used to mean "you can act here." Never add a second decorative accent color.

rules.name: The Status-Means-Something Rule
rules.body: Red, green, and amber never appear as decoration — only as danger/success/partial state.

rules.name: The No-Display-Font Rule
rules.body: This system has no hero typography. A page title at 24px/700 is the largest text anywhere.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow only separates a floating element (modal, dropdown, toast) from the page beneath it.

dos: Do keep Working Blue as the only action-accent color.
Do use the -soft tint + -deep/-hover text pairing for any new status badge.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every new animation.
Do anchor any new popover/dropdown/tooltip to its trigger with a scale+fade entrance.
Do use the system sans stack and the four-step type scale for any new screen.
donts: Don't add a second decorative accent color alongside Working Blue.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text or a gradient hero background.
Don't introduce bouncy/elastic easing or celebratory motion.
Don't give any page a display-sized headline above 24px/700.
Don't stack a shadow and a border on the same card.
